cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about the study by the World Bank in 1974 regarding economic growth in some developing countries and what it brought. First, I'll need to refer back to the provided text. The text says: "A major study by the World Bank in 1974 concluded that it is now clear that more than decades of rapid growth in developing countries has been of little benefit to a third of their population." So the key sentence here is that the study concluded that "more than decades of rapid growth... has been of little benefit to a third of their population." That suggests that a third of the population got little benefit from this growth. So if there's a third who got little benefit, that implies that the rest (the other two thirds) might have had more benefit, or maybe not. But the question is about what the study found regarding the benefit from economic growth.
